Figure 5 compares ZTD calculated by the GPS-BDS-3
combination of PPP-B2b at JFNG, URUM, and WUH2 sites
regarding their scatter plots and relationship (correlations).
Overall, the ZTD calculated by the GPS-BDS-3 combina-
tion of PPP-B2b correlates with CNES for the three stations
(Fig. 5). The correlation coefficients (R) of all three stations
exceed 0.90, indicating a strong correlation between PPP-
B2b and

3 22 Earth Science Informatics (2023) 16:315–328
Fig. 7 Differences of T m
between GPT3 and ERA5
exceeds 70 mm at DOY 200. The rise of PWV at the Hong
Kong Observatory meteorological station may be associated
with heavy rainfall. Ignoring the distance bias, the PWV of
HKSL and HKWS from PPP-B2b also reflects this rising
trend. This shows the potential of the PPP-B2b service for
PWV retrieval, rainfall forecasting, and other meteorologi-
cal monitoring.
Figure 11 shows the retrieval PWV of GPS/BDS-3 by
IGS stations. CNES has slightly better accuracy and con-
vergence time than PPP-B2b. For the

Due to the lack of meteorological sensors to provide true
PWV values over the ship, the PWV of the coordinates esti-
mated by real-time PPP was calculated by ERA5 as a refer-
ence. The positioning accuracy of real-time PPP is usually in
